steps.title: Identify the key structural pattern
steps.content: The passage notes a discrepancy: historians of capitalism implicitly present capitalism as imposed on Africa, whereas Africanist scholars have shown it predated colonization. The passage then explains this discrepancy through a methodological limitation—historians rely on archival records that don't exist for precolonial Africa.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B states the passage's purpose: to present and account for a potentially misleading characteristic of historians' work—and the passage does exactly that. The other choices either reduce the issue to mere disagreement (A), focus on the wrong group (C), or misframe the problem as one of data interpretation rather than availability (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
